_comment: Cross-problem evolution memory. Every time the metabolism evolves a function, the winning pattern is stored here. The memory layer reads this before generating new implementations to avoid 2-3 generations of rediscovery.

evolutions.description: Removed 202 'as any' casts by introducing proper Supabase type generics. Pattern: replace `(await supabase.from('table').select('*')) as any` with `supabase.from('table').select('*').returns<TableRow[]>()`. 5 incremental commits, each passing quality gates.
evolutions.approach: File-by-file, largest offenders first. Each file committed independently.
evolutions.lesson: Type safety fixes are high-success-rate experiments. Target files with 5+ casts first for maximum impact per experiment.

evolutions.description: Created typed `fromTable` helper that wraps Supabase queries with generics, eliminating 20 more 'as any' casts in one commit. Pattern: `const { data } = await fromTable('projects').select('*')` returns typed data.
evolutions.approach: Created a shared utility first, then replaced all callers. Batch replacement after utility was tested.
evolutions.lesson: Creating a shared utility FIRST then replacing callers is more reliable than fixing each file independently. The utility becomes a force multiplier.

evolutions.description: Added Zod schemas, error boundaries, and mutation error handlers across the app. Zod validates API responses at runtime, error boundaries catch render failures, mutation handlers show user-friendly errors.
evolutions.metric_before: No runtime validation, white-screen on errors
evolutions.metric_after: 97 files touched, runtime validation on critical paths
evolutions.approach: Batch implementation in a single commit — error boundaries wrap route-level components, Zod schemas co-located with API hooks.
evolutions.lesson: Error handling infrastructure should be implemented as cross-cutting concerns (wrapping routers, wrapping mutations) rather than per-component. One well-placed ErrorBoundary at the route level catches everything below it.

evolutions.description: Added permission gates to Schedule, FieldCapture, Directory, and Files pages. Pattern: check user role before rendering sensitive actions, show appropriate empty/forbidden state.
evolutions.metric_before: All actions visible to all users
evolutions.metric_after: Permission-gated UI on 4 pages (311 file changes)
evolutions.approach: Created a PermissionGate component, then wrapped each sensitive action.
evolutions.lesson: Permission UI is a FEATURE experiment that demos well for enterprise buyers. GCs always ask 'can I control who sees what?' High demo value.

killed_approaches.description: Batch refactors across 10+ files in one commit. The immune system (homeostasis) rejects these at Tier 2 because too many concurrent changes can't be verified individually.
killed_approaches.alternative: One file per commit. Each commit must pass quality gates independently.

killed_approaches.description: Adding TODO stubs or empty functions. The quality auditor scores incomplete code WORSE than no code. Never ship stubs.
killed_approaches.alternative: Implement completely or don't implement at all. Half-implementations degrade quality scores.

killed_approaches.description: Using npm ci with Mac-generated lockfiles on Linux CI. Vite 8's rolldown requires platform-specific native bindings. The lockfile from Mac doesn't include Linux bindings.
killed_approaches.alternative: Always use 'rm -f package-lock.json && npm install' in CI.

patterns.name: Cursor-based pagination
patterns.description: Always use cursor-based pagination for lists. Never offset-based. Cursor: use the last item's created_at + id as the cursor. This avoids the 'page drift' problem when items are added while paginating.
patterns.applies_to: any list query with more than 50 items

patterns.name: Optimistic update with rollback
patterns.description: For mutations: 1) save previousData with queryClient.getQueryData, 2) optimistically update with queryClient.setQueryData, 3) onError: restore previousData and show toast, 4) onSettled: invalidate to get server truth. Never skip the rollback.

patterns.name: Typed Supabase helper (fromTable)
patterns.description: Use the fromTable<T>('tableName') helper from src/lib/supabase.ts for all database queries. It returns typed data without 'as any' casts. Never call supabase.from() directly without type generics.

patterns.name: Route-level ErrorBoundary
patterns.description: Wrap each route in an ErrorBoundary component that catches render errors and shows a user-friendly fallback UI. One ErrorBoundary per route, not per component.

patterns.name: RLS with (select auth.uid()) wrapper
patterns.description: All RLS policies MUST wrap auth.uid() in a subselect: USING (user_id = (select auth.uid())). This prevents per-row re-evaluation and gives a 1,571x performance improvement on large tables.
